evals.prompt: Fullstack project: React + Node API + PostgreSQL. Running /forge-team. Platform detection finds all 3. Walk me through waves, parallel spawning, and dependency blocking.
evals.expected_output: Wave 0: DB validator spawned (alone, foundation layer). Waits for PASS. Wave 1: API validator spawned (depends on DB PASS). Waits for PASS. Wave 2: Web validator spawned (depends on API PASS). If DB FAILs in Wave 0 → API and Web are BLOCKED (never spawned). Records BLOCKED in their reports. If API FAILs in Wave 1 → Web is BLOCKED. Lead writes final report aggregating all waves with dependency chain and root causes.

evals.prompt: Web validator reports CONDITIONAL (3/5 journeys PASS, 2/5 FAIL—API timeouts on pagination endpoint). API validator is already done (Wave 1). iOS validator is waiting for Wave 2. What happens?
evals.expected_output: Wave 1 complete with CONDITIONAL verdict. Lead evaluates: API has known failures, but core CRUD works. Adds warning to iOS validator prompt: 'Upstream Wave 1: API verdict is CONDITIONAL. Use only PASS endpoints (list, get, create, update). Avoid pagination endpoint (times out). Evidence captured in e2e-evidence/api/report.md.' iOS proceeds (not blocked), focuses on endpoints that passed upstream.
